title: MCP HttpTransport drops response id mismatch detection
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/mcp.rs:298-338 (HttpTransport::request)
## Violation
`StdioTransport::request` validates that `response.id == id` (line 243-248) to detect protocol desync. The HTTP transport allocates and increments `request_id` (line 299) and embeds it in the JSON-RPC request — but never compares it against `response.id`. A buggy or hostile MCP HTTP server can return a response correlated to a different request and the client will silently accept it, returning the wrong tool's result to the caller.

```rust
let response: JsonRpcResponse = response
    .json()
    .await
    .map_err(|e| McpError::Protocol(format!("Failed to parse response: {e}")))?;

if let Some(error) = response.error {
    return Err(McpError::Protocol(format!(
        "RPC error {}: {}",
        error.code, error.message
    )));
}

Ok(response.result.unwrap_or(Value::Null))
```

The `id` field on `JsonRpcResponse` is parsed and discarded.
## Mandated refactor
After deserializing the response in `HttpTransport::request`, add the same id-mismatch guard as `StdioTransport`. Better, extract the request/response correlation into a shared helper to enforce DRY across transports.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
